creator: sblachmann@gmail.com
text:
Thank you Robert for the hint!

That is bad, but likely a result of being bent on using shell script instead of a programming language.

Until this is done, I guess the proper solution is write/use a subroutine/function to handle what was intended to handle with sysrc and no longer use sysrc.

I will do so, and leave this PR up as a reminder that the sysrc script should be replaced with a proper program that is not affected by shell limitations and side effects.
